name: Roast Leg of Lamb with Rhubarb Mint Chutney
recipeInstructions:
1. Use fresh or frozen, short-cut leg of lamb. If using frozen, thaw in the refrigerator overnight.
2. Trim any excess fat from the lamb and make several slits in the meaty side.
3. Stir together oil, half the mint, and half the lemon zest in a bowl.
4. Rub the mixture over the entire leg, ensuring it goes into the slits. The leg can be prepared to this point, covered, and refrigerated for up to one day.
5. To make Rhubarb Mint Chutney:
a. Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat; cook onion for 3 minutes or until soft.
b. Add rhubarb, sugar, lemon juice, remaining mint, and remaining lemon zest.
c. Bring to boil, stirring to dissolve sugar. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for 5 to 7 minutes or until thickened.
d. Season to taste with salt and pepper. The chutney can be prepared, cooled, covered, and refrigerated for up to 2 days.
6. Place the lamb on a rack in a shallow roasting pan; roast, uncovered, in a 450°F (230°C) oven for 15 minutes.
7. Reduce temperature to 325°F (160°C) and continue roasting for about 1 hour and 15 minutes longer or until internal temperature is 130°F (54°C) to 140°F (60°C) on a meat thermometer.
8. Remove the lamb and let rest under tent of foil for 15 minutes before carving.
9. Accompany with Rhubarb Mint Chutney to serve.
